strategy: OBSERVE — Stock trading above analyst targets; wait for target revisions

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $290.52 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$29,100 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.

recommendation.title: High-Risk Speculative — Options Too Expensive at Current IV
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ NOT RECOMMENDED at current levels. IV is ~117% — options cost 20% of stock price. More critically: the stock at $290 is ABOVE the average analyst target of $195. Most analyst targets predate the Oracle 2.8GW deal (April 27) and have not been updated. Wait for analyst target revisions before entering options.

exitPlan.label: Preferred
exitPlan.optionGain: Re-evaluate when IV drops and targets update

companyOverview: Fuel cell maker turned AI data center power partner. Q1 2026 revenue $751M (+130% YoY) with non-GAAP EPS $0.44 (beat by 340%). Oracle partnership expanded to 2.8GW (Project Jupiter) — a 10-year contract worth potentially billions. AEP 1GW agreement also signed. 2026 guidance raised to $3.4–3.8B revenue (+80% YoY). Manufacturing capacity expanding to 2GW. Stock up 134% YTD — largely priced in the Oracle deal at current levels.

financials.note: Extremely elevated — ATM call costs ~20% of stock
BELOW current price — most targets pre-Oracle deal

catalysts: Oracle 2.8GW fuel cell partnership — 10-year multi-billion contract (Project Jupiter)
Q1 2026 results: 130% revenue growth, first Q1 positive operating cash flow
Manufacturing scale-up to 2GW supporting demand surge
Expected analyst target revisions post-Oracle deal announcement
risks: Stock at $290 trades ABOVE average analyst target of $195 — most targets pre-date Oracle deal
IV at 117% makes options extremely expensive (~20% of stock price per contract)
Manufacturing scale-up to 2GW is unproven at this speed
City of Hilliard (OH) legal opposition to large fuel cell installations
Hyperscaler capex pullback would directly impact key customers
Concentration risk: Oracle represents huge portion of backlog
Natural gas price volatility impacts fuel cell economics
dilution: Rapid expansion funded by equity raises and debt. Watch for potential capital raise to fund 2GW manufacturing capacity expansion. Stock-based compensation elevated relative to earnings.
